hcd_data.architectural_style: House-form, Semi-detached, Bay-and-Gable (confirmed – HCD Plan Vol. 2)
hcd_data.statement_of_contribution: Each property has value as a late-nineteenth- to early-twentieth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Victorian architectural styles. Through its construction date and typology, each property is directly associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s. Constructed as a pair, the properties retain most of their original building features, including cross-gabled rooflines, mirrored facades, double-height bay windows, brick cladding with decorative brickwork, and fenestration pattern with mix of three- centred and flat arch openings. Contextually, each property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street. Each property supports and maintains the residential character of the streetscape and is functionally, visually, and historically linked to the District.

context.street_character: One-way EB | 40 km/h | Sub-standard sidewalks | Mixed commercial-residential

facade_detail.composition: The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its semi-detached bay-and-gable house-form typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 2 window bays from lower to upper levels, using double-hung sash windows, and 1 principal entrance.
facade_detail.heritage_expression: Character-defining facade elements include decorative brick, original windows, bay window.
facade_detail.heritage_summary: Constructed as a pair, the properties retain most of their original building features, including cross-gabled rooflines, mirrored facades, double-height bay windows, brick cladding with decorative brickwork, and fenestration pattern with mix of three- centred and flat arch openings. Contextually, each property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street.

facade_description: Semi-detached bay-and-gable house (pre-1889) in red-brown brick with decorative brickwork and dark trim. Two storeys under a cross-gable, with a three-sided projecting bay spanning both floors. Fair condition with some missing elements. Party walls on both sides; single dwelling unit. High lot coverage (72%) with a rear chimney.
heritage_summary: Constructed as a pair, the properties retain most of their original building features, including cross-gabled rooflines, mirrored facades, double-height bay windows, brick cladding with decorative brickwork, and fenestration pattern with mix of three- centred and flat arch openings. Contextually, each property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street.
